negative_cases.why_wrong: Analog Primitive Isource Instance: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to Instantiate the current-source primitive as an internal analog primitive.

Analog Primitive Isource Instance: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Instantiate a Spectre analog current-source primitive inside a Verilog-A module fail.
Analog Primitive Isource Instance: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Analog Primitive Isource Instance: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for Instantiate the current-source primitive as an internal analog primitive fails.

Analog Primitive Isource Instance: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Instantiate a Spectre analog current-source primitive inside a Verilog-A module fail.
